I couldn&rsquo;t run around the block to save my life, I was always the last one to be picked for any athletic event, and I was tired all the time. In my early 20s I got tired of feeling tired and being &ldquo;skinny fat.&rdquo; I looked thin but my body fat was high, and I constantly complained about feeling tired. So, since I didn&rsquo;t have a natural athletic cell in my body, I started to study the science of exercise (way before it was in vogue) and learn how I could make adaptations at the cellular level so that I could do more. Slowly but surely, I got myself fit. I was so stoked about how I felt and looked that I got my teaching certification from AFFA and ACE and started teaching aerobics at a big club in Atlanta. My class grew and so did my interest in changing bodies by applying exercise science. I have experienced first hand what it takes to transform ones body. I have been overweight and had to completely change my lifestyle to redefine my physique and create a healthy habit of living. I have an extensive knowledge of vitamins and supplements that I gained while working in the supplement industry. I also have 2 years of nursing experience which has helped me learn the safest and most effective ways to train the human body. I will help you work on changing your diet into a healthier and leaner daily dietary routine as well as create a custom workout program for you that will evolve as you evolve. If you are serious about making a change in your body and lifestyle or are just trying to train for your upcoming athletics season, I would love to help you along that path.</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>I am very adaptable with my training styles. My skin tells my story and with my story, I help others believe in themselves...\n+Read More\n\nMy name is Constantin Wohlgemuth, also known as The Inked Coach. Why The Inked Coach? Tattoos were the meaning behind my experiences and grief. My skin tells my story and with my story, I help others believe in themselves the way I did. With my introduction, I would like to break the ice between us and welcome you with open arms, to build trust.<br><br>I was born in Germany in November 1988 and it is since then my story begins. At the age of 17 years old I had to decide to say goodbye to my alcohol and drug addiction. It was then that I decided to change my life for good. At the age of 17, I began therapy for a few years and experienced a lot of losses and dark moments. I was able to see light at the end of the tunnel and pushed my way forward with the help of friends and family. I dived deep into my darkest corners and healed myself from within. It was then that Sport and the right nutrition became my biggest focus to help me get back on my feet and gave me the discipline and stamina to keep going and take care of my Internal health as well as my external.<br>Throughout my journey, I decided to help other people change their lives too. I’ve helped hundreds of people change their lives and continue to do so! It takes courage, perseverance, and, time because it is an endless journey. We often believe we are running out of time or that we have the feeling we are running out of time but, that only happens when we are not present and we are living either in the past or future. Possibly experiencing fear is one part and ego another part that may be contributing to our story. I will help you to rediscover yourself and to unfold your potential, whatever it is that has always been within you. We were not born with fear, pain, or the thought of failure. We were born to make the best of our lives and help others to do the same and, that’s why only a healthy mind can survive in a healthy body.<br><br>One of my biggest challenges was moving from Germany to the United States during the corona pandemic in 2021. I don’t think I have ever mastered so many challenges in such a short time as I did during this time.<br><br>Today I live with my beautiful wife in this beautiful sunshine state of Florida, where we met in 2016. After almost 4 years of a long-distance relationship, we decided to get married on the beach where we met and now, we build a life together. It’s hard to leave family and friends behind, but it’s worth standing up and investing for love and your needs in life, no matter how scared you are of the unknown. You never know what life has in store for you. We only fail if we don’t try and give it our all. It is up to us whether we live our lives differently today or whether we give our habits the power to decide our actions. Life does not begin on the day you are born, but on the day, you decide to live your other life.\n\nNutrition\n\nWeight Loss\n\nMuscle Gain\n\nFunctional Training\n\nGerman\n\nBeginners\n\nBodybuilders\n\nOverweight/ Obese\n\nAddiction\n\nAnkle Injury\n\nAnxiety\n\nAsthma\n\nBack Pain/Issues\n\nDiabetes\n\nEating Disorders\n\nHigh Cholesterol\n\nLimited Mobility\n\nLower Body Pain/Issues\n\nPrediabetes\n\nStress\n\nFigure/Bodybuilding Competitions\n\nWeddings\n\nPersonal training A license Fitness training B license Medical fitness training A license Back\n\nGraduated and licensed sports and health trainer\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$99\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nMy name is Constantin Wohlgemuth, also known as The Inked Coach. While getting his degree in Health Sciences from Drexel University, Petros took note of his passion of truly helping people, whether it was in reaching their fitness goals or helping them overcome an injury. Once his tenure at UPenn was over, he moved onto working in injury, post-op and sports rehabilitation at NY Rehab and then after at STARS (Sports Therapy and Rehabilitation Services) at North Shore LIJ in East Meadow, NY. <br /><br />Through his various experiences Petros took note that many people always found an excuse as to why they were not able to get to the gym, whether it was the time, the cost, the inconvenience. I focus on injury prevention and pain management (then we move into #beastmode, or maybe #wegotoffthecouchmode) I train virtually and in-person. My vibe is encouraging, understanding, and trauma-informed.</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>With trauma-informed coaching I am attuned to sensory needs, fight/flight/fawn responses, and triggers. I will always listen and respect what is said about feelings and needs. We will work on specific exercises based on your body&rsquo;s responses (including fight/flight/fawn), and eventually this can push your window of tolerance. This means, we will create a safe space and when you&rsquo;re ready, use exercises to expand your ability to handle stress without slipping into old patterns.</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>I offer training virtually 1:1, group workouts, and group coaching. This can make putting strength and muscle on your frame not only difficult but impossible for some.</span></p><br><p style=\"line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; background-color: transparent; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-position: normal; vertical-align: baseline; white-space-collapse: preserve;\">&nbsp;</span></p><br><p style=\"line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; background-color: transparent; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-position: normal; vertical-align: baseline; white-space-collapse: preserve;\">Without this protective layer of muscle (and bone density) it makes men at risk for developing a host of other problems, including increased injury susceptibility. We don't have the motivation, the drive, the recovery ability, and frankly: working out hard is now unpleasant. I'm a trainer specializing in those who want to get in shape and live a healthy lifestyle for the long term.</p><br><p>If this is you, then read on:</p><br><p>My approach is progressive training. There's an initial fitness assessment and medical history that takes place during the first session. The first session is 1.5 hours long but you only pay for the hour.&nbsp;</p><br><p>I start from where you are now (taking into consideration any injuries, or medical issues such as high blood, pressure, diabetes, etc.) to get you from point A to point B. As you master each step, I up the difficulty so you are always gaining results. The routines are varied as we continue and includes, warm up, strength training, aerobic endurance,&nbsp; rehab exercises if needed along with a 10 minutes stretching and cool down to increase range of motion and flexibility.</p><br><p>Although I can train clients from 19-90 years old, my main target market are those ages 35 -70 who are serious about their health. These people can be those who don't fit the \"general workout routines\" due to injuries (elbow, shoulder, lower back or knees perhaps??) and don't know HOW they can workout without hurting themselves.</p><br><p>They can be those needing motivation especially with new workouts geared to the 35+. year old body. They come to realize that weight does not come off as easily as it did in their 20's. They can be those needing accountability so as not to neglect their health while tending to their careers and many responsibilities.&nbsp;</p><br><p>I offer remote online training so I can reach more people. You don't need a lot of equipment so don't think, \"Oh no, I don't have gym set up in my house!\" There are ways to work out your body with equipment at hand (furniture, table, body resistance) and if you wish, the few pieces of equipment (should you wish to buy them) are not expensive.&nbsp;</p><br><p>As for me, I've been a certified master trainer since 2003. I have trained clients from 19-90, received certifications in stretching techniques and rehab, and have come across all types of people with all types of different personalities and fitness levels.&nbsp;</p><br><p>What got me started as a trainer? My own health and wellbeing took a hit at the ripe age of 26. Formally a track and basketball player, I found myself huffing and puffing after only walking a couple of blocks. I was a mom of 3 by this time.&nbsp; I decided to back into shape to feel better and as a result it not only got me in shape, but was a great stress outlet due to those lovely endorphins. It stuck.</p><br><p>I love to teach and I love exercise so I combined the two to become a fitness trainer to help others.&nbsp;</p><br><p>Ask me about an eating plan that is easy to follow, that doesn't have you buying tons of ingredients, but enables you to lose weight naturally!</p><br><p>This is offered as an accountability eating program. We (you, the new client, and other clients wanting to be on the program, and myself as well) meet weekly to go over how we are doing each week.&nbsp;</p><br><p>The eating plan offer are only for my clients who sign up for training with me.&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p>\n\nCore and Balance\n\nNutrition\n\nStrength Training\n\nWeight Loss\n\nFlexibility and Mobility\n\nMuscle Gain\n\nCardiovascular Conditioning\n\nInjury Prevention\n\nWellness\n\nMen\n\nMiddle-aged\n\nOverweight/ Obese\n\nPost-rehab\n\nSeniors\n\nWomen\n\nAsthma\n\nBack Pain/Issues\n\nBone/Joint Injury\n\nDiabetes\n\nHigh Blood Pressure\n\nHypertension\n\nLimited Mobility\n\nPrediabetes\n\nRotator Cuff Issues\n\nTennis Elbow\n\nObstacle Courses\n\nRunning Races (Marathons)\n\nACE Special Populations\n\nNPTI Master Personal Trainer\n\nCPR/AED/First Aid\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$29\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nHello there! I am able to service a very wide range of clients from beginners to advanced lifters to post-rehab. My sessions are typically focused on making sure the exercises are catered to YOUR mobility/flexibility and I will never put you in a position that you do not \"own.\" Meaning that if your arm can't go overhead without compensating, there is no world where I would ever give you a dumbbell for an overhead press. Sounds like a no brainer but you'd be surprised at what you see at the gym. <br><br>I value mobility and proper movement over maximal effort, but that doesn't mean I can't give you a good kick in the pants! I try to educate as much as I can during my sessions so that you are learning the \"why\" for the movements I am asking you to do. Realistically, I can't be with you for every workout so I want you to have the confidence to know that what you are doing isn't going to hurt you if you go to the gym on your own. If you are just trying to get your butt whooped with no regard of form, then I am not the trainer for you. Growing up as a dancer, gymnast, and springboard diver, Ali discovered that movement was more than just fun and competition&mdash;it became a powerful tool for healing and self-expression.</p><br><p style=\"white-space: pre-wrap; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto; text-align: center;\">After earning her BA in Dance Choreography and Performance, Ali found her next passion in yoga, becoming a certified Vinyasa instructor in 2015. Her journey continued as she studied under three of Manhattan's top personal trainers, leading to her NASM Personal Training certification. Always eager to learn, Ali has since earned certifications in TRX and Silver Sneakers, become a Corrective Exercise Specialist, and has most recently graduated from a 14-month course to become a Somatic educator.</p><br><p style=\"white-space: pre-wrap; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto; text-align: center;\">As a functional trainer, Ali's mission is to make movement fun, effective, and educational for each client. That’s what all these great trainers and coaches on FYT are here for.  How do you know which one to hire? T...\n+Read More\n\n<div>Whether a little or a lot, you could use some help with your fitness, health, and nutrition. That&rsquo;s what all these great trainers and coaches on FYT are here for.&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>How do you know which one to hire?</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>That&rsquo;s up to you but I can tell you why you would or would not want to hire me.&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>Let's start with why you wouldn&rsquo;t want to hire me.&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><ol><br><li>You're an athlete, model, or person who has it all together but are looking for a small boost to be elite.&nbsp;</li><br><li>You&rsquo;re looking for a drill sergeant to make you do burpees till you puke.&nbsp;</li><br><li>You want a trainer with a bubbly personality that is packed full of high fives to give you for everything you do.&nbsp;</li><br></ol><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>The three points above describe most of my industry.&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>Most trainers are athletes who have spent a lifetime developing the skills they need to have the body and athleticism they possess. They usually get into the industry to work with people similar to themselves. Most people who hire trainers though don&rsquo;t have similar backgrounds or goals and this becomes a tension point in the relationship and affects the quality of the coaching. &nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>Two, anyone can beat you up but can they make you better? Ask anyone and they&rsquo;ll tell you that going to extremes is ineffective in the long run but then they turn around and do workouts that leave them on the floor half dead. Changing your body requires hard work, but more than that it requires smart work.&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>Finally, it's important in a coaching relationship that your personalities match. Many people do better with a coach that&rsquo;s bubbly and always excited. Some would rather their coach be more reserved and thoughtful. I&rsquo;m definitely the second one and you should know that when making your choice.&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>With that in mind, who should hire me?</div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><ol><br><li>Regular people who want to get in better shape and stay in better shape.&nbsp;</li><br><li>Older people who are trying to keep their fitness in line with their busy schedules</li><br><li>People who are really struggling and need guidance.&nbsp;</li><br><li>People who are ready to give up on themselves because the thought of trying to improve is just too daunting.&nbsp;</li><br></ol><br><div>&nbsp;</div><br><div>&nbsp;I like to work with real people who are just trying to make their lives and health better. My name is Hailee and I am an NASM certified personal trainer! What makes me unique as a trainer is that I am mobile. This means I bring the workout to you! I show up at your front door with my mat, foam roller, and w...\n+Read More\n\n<p>Hi! My name is Hailee and I am an NASM certified personal trainer! What makes me unique as a trainer is that I am mobile. This means I bring the workout to you! I show up at your front door with my mat, foam roller, and whatever training equipment we need for your session that day. My car is full of dumbbells, medicine balls, kettlebells, resistance bands, stability balls, and so much more! The reason I like to keep so much equipment on hand is so no two workouts are the same. I like to switch up your workouts constantly, in order to keep the body guessing and promote weight loss. Each and every workout will be created just for you and I like to keep open lines of communication so I can find out what kinds of workouts you prefer. I like to know if you feel the workout was too hard, too easy, too complicated, etc. This is important to me because I want to find types of workouts you can enjoy (I know, \"enjoy\" is a relative term) so that you are more likely to meet your health and fitness goals in between our workout sessions. My goal as a trainer is to teach you the skills you need to create your own workouts and show you that you don't need a gym membership or fancy fitness studio to get in great shape! I like to get my clients to the point that they don't need a trainer because they know exactly what to do to keep meeting their fitness goals; although I also love when clients want to work with me long-term.</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>Depending on your goals, I typically give \"homework\" in between training sessions which usually includes workouts on your own time. I will tell you exactly what to do so that you aren't left guessing. I also offer nutritional guidance because nutrition is a HUGE part of weight loss. I like to take a look at your current diet and eating habits and help you to make healthy and sustainable changes. I usually prefer to make small changes one at a time so that you are more likely to be able to make these changes permanent. If you would prefer to reform your whole diet at once, we can do that as well; I have just found that it is usually more successful to do a little at a time. I also don't believe in completely depriving yourself of certain foods you love. I prefer moderation, healthy choices more than 80% of the time, and expending more calories than you take in.</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>I would love the opportunity to tell you more about my training philosophies and myself! We could sit down for a consultation so I can learn more about you and your goals and you can decide if you think we are a good fit. Please feel free to let me know if you have any questions or woud like to schedule a consultation! I am certified personal trainer, &nbsp;my passion for helping people be the best version of themselves has been there my whole life. Growing up, I was always given the label \"strong\", when really my realtionship with fitness and nutrition were weak. I was so fixated on keeping that label that I did not really care about my apprach to fitness. Thereofore, I could only improve/achieve so much, I always came to this stopping point, no matter how many times I tried to get past that stopping point. Whenever people would ask me, \"How many times a week do you workout,\" \"when do you wake up to workout,\" \"Did you workout yet,\" no matter who they were, if I got a weird vibe then I would literally lie or come up with an excuse because of my guilt. I became a Health Coach because I get to focus on all the aspects of a healthful life: sleep, nutrition and stress management along with movement. I was a personal trainer and group fitness instructor for many years. Turns out, it's not just about movement. When you move more, you're going to be hungrier. What and when will you eat? When you're moving more, you need more rest. How and when will you get this? Life is always changing. Sometimes when stress is high, we need to cut back our movement and do other things that make us feel revitalized like: sitting in silence, reading a book or catching up with a friend.&nbsp;</p><br><p>Together, we unpack the nitty, gritty juicy parts of your daily routine, and figure out the best way for you to make deeply rooted change by taking into acoount your values, barriers, and social support. A typical session lasts anywhere from 30-60 minutes. We meet virtually, in a safe, non-judgemental space with the common goal of reaching your health, and ultimately life goals.&nbsp;</p><br><p>Health is wealth. Without it, we miss out on a lot of life. It costs money and time to be sick. Time that you could be spending with your loved ones, and money you could be investing in your future. Bottom line, prevention is always better then a cure. Unfortunately, our health system is made to treat illness. The problem is, sometimes it's too late by the time we're already sick. Let's partner together, and pave a better way of living for you and your family.&nbsp;</p><br><p>A lot of times we use our families and responsibilites as reasons we don't take care of ourselves. Marathons...\n+Read More\n\n<p>Finishing your first marathon, half marathon or 5K can be one of the greatest experiences and accomplishments of your life. The road to this accomplishment can be challenging and can often be derailed by injury. Marathons, half-marathons, 5Ks and other extreme endurance sports require higher volumes of training that can often aggravate pre-existing postural and muscular imbalances. Fortunately, these injuries can often be prevented through a personalized, proactive muscular endurance, strengthening and flexibility program that works in concert with your cardiovascular training to help you arrive at the start line both fit and injury-free. Each program includes: - regular strength, flexibility and balance training sessions - endurance training program design, monitoring and adjustment as needed - nutrition guidance for peak performance and/or weight loss Each program is tailored for the individual. Laurie Lasseter is uniquely qualified to work with you to create such a program and achieve your endurance event goals. With over 28 years of Fitness Industry experience and over 35 years of Endurance Running experience, completing 30 marathons (including 3 Boston Marathons and all 6 World Marathon Majors) and numerous other distance events, Laurie has used these methods to help clients achieve injury-free marathon completions, marathon time goals and Boston-Qualifying marathon efforts. Training injuries can often be prevented through a personalized, proactive muscular endurance, strengthening and flexibility program that works in concert with your cardiovascular training to help you arrive at the start line of your event both fit and injury-free.</p>\n\nStrength Training\n\nWeight Loss\n\nFunctional Training\n\nRunning Races (Marathons)\n\nAFAA Certified Personal Trainer\n\nACE Certified Personal Trainer\n\nACE Certified Group Fitness Instructor\n\nRRCA Certified Running Coach\n\nBSEE, BSCSc, MSEE\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$69\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nFinishing your first marathon, half marathon or 5K can be one of the greatest experiences and accomplishments of your life. I continued various forms of dance throughout my childhood and then switched to cardio and weights in my teen years. I did learn the hard way in my early 20s when I started working a desk job, eating out, and not exercising regularly:</p><br><p style=\"font-size: 16.3814px; color: rgba(11, 72, 91, 0.75); font-family: 'Work Sans'; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1.6em; white-space: pre-wrap; overflow-wrap: break-word;\"><strong>&ldquo;I lost a great job and my aunt to cancer both in late 2006; my body was so stressed out and I did not feel like myself. It was a tough time in my life and I knew I had to take the reins myself and transform my health.&rdquo;</strong></p><br><p style=\"font-size: 16.3814px; color: rgba(11, 72, 91, 0.75); font-family: 'Work Sans'; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1.6em; white-space: pre-wrap; overflow-wrap: break-word;\">I realized I had to make a change and joined a women&rsquo;s only gym where I returned to cardio and weightlifting. I started cooking more nutritious food and have continued experimenting with different exercise programs. With my ballet background, I am big on form and function when exercising and making sure clients are safe from injury or prolonged pain. I have personally dealt with feet and knee injuries and also suffered from migraines for 2 years.</p><br><p style=\"font-size: 16.3814px; color: rgba(11, 72, 91, 0.75); font-family: 'Work Sans'; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1.6em; white-space: pre-wrap; overflow-wrap: break-word;\">As an adult, my passion for fitness stems from the many friends and relatives I have lost to diseases and people in my life who struggle with daily setbacks from their condition, whether chronic or unattended. I took 72 hours of class time and became an ACE-Certifiedin December 2016 after my desire to obtain the certification for a few years finally came to fruition.</p><br><p style=\"font-size: 16.3814px; color: rgba(11, 72, 91, 0.75); font-family: 'Work Sans'; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1.6em; white-space: pre-wrap; overflow-wrap: break-word;\">In June 2018, I completed the extensive Certified Pre- and Postnatal Coaching from Girls Gone Strong&rsquo;s <strong>Coaching and Training Women Academy</strong> which I renewed again in 2020. In June of 2022, I renewed this certification with their latest curriculum.<br /><br />In addition, I completed a trauma-informed space holding in 2020 as I felt called to learn how to hold a safe and supportive environment for my clients regardless of race, sexual orientation, body type, gender, or any lived experience my clients may have. I understand I hold privilege as a white, heterosexual &amp; cis-gender woman and that it is critical to be empathetic and understanding without implicit bias or bypassing trauma to those who are not in my same position nor have had the opportunities that have been extended to me. We will then move into a workout that addresses your unique goals and needs. After having done traditional personal training for several years, I&nbsp; discovered my love and interest in neuromuscular therapies, pain dysfunctions and how to approach them. I have studied Dynamic Neuromuscular Stabilization (DNS), which focuses on diaphragmatic breathing and posture, and I hold certifications in Neurokinetic Therapy (Level 3), Proprioceptive Deep Tendon Reflex, and Cranials-Opening Space. I'm not only interested in helping clients become stronger and losing weight, but more importantly how to move efficiently, effectively, and without pain. By combining strength and conditioning exercises, kettlebell training, and primal movement drills in my work outs, I am giving my clients the ability to move better and pain free, as well as rehabbing Local training&nbsp;</p><br><p><br />Hope Nagy&rsquo;s love of fitness is evident on both a professional and personal level. Coupled with her nearly 20 years of experience as a fitness professional is Hope&rsquo;s dedication to fitness and a healthy lifestyle. A Certified Personal Trainer and group fitness instructor, Hope devotes herself to helping clients change their habits, change their bodies and change their lives. She discovered her love for fitness while in college at West Chester University, when she became the mascot for a professional football team. At the core of Hope&rsquo;s personal and professional success is her belief that there are absolutely no limits except for the ones that we place on ourselves, and that reaching our fitness goals improves all areas of our lives. Hope has a no-excuse style, a positive and motivating personality, and a passion for helping others meet their goals, all of which makes her a highly sought-after personal trainer. Hope is the founder of MotivateHopeStrength Personal Training, and was named Philly&rsquo;s Top Personal Trainer multiple times since 2011 in Philly&rsquo;s Annual Hot List and Montgomery County PA&rsquo;s Born and raised in Philadelphia, Hope is proud to be a contributor to many health &amp; fitness publications including her personal fitness blog at on wordpress and she can be found on social media pages where she loves to share her fitspiration. She is also very involved in many local charity events such as The Philly Spin In for The Children's Hospital of Philadelphia, and SHOW US YOUR CANS -Turkey Bootcamp for Philabundance and is also a volunteer for her local Police Department. Hope is a passionate person who believes that everyday you have a choice to be happy or miserable. Hope has a lot to be happy for everyday. As a trainer and one who promoted the importance of taking care of ones body, Hope learned in 2016 she was born with a congenital heart defect and would need open heart surgery for a new Aortic Valve. Two weeks later she was back in the gym reminding her clients that this is why we train.... for life unexpected surprises. So when cancer came knocking soon after, Hope won that battle and once again proved that her strong mind and body can not only win that fight but work while doing it too. Ask her clients who never seem to leave her. It's hard to find an excuse when your trainer came to train you from her radiation treatment. Life is a gift to Hope and exercise is a present we give ourselves each day.&nbsp;</p><br><p>&nbsp;</p><br><p>Virtual training has always allowed Hope to connect with clients from all over. Now more than ever she has been able with her virtual experience keep her in person clients still physically and mentally in shape through these tough times. &nbsp;As a&nbsp;trainer she wants to educate you on how to exercise and use proper form. She was not taught at a chain style gym where they like to confuse the client to rely on the trainer. Her goal is to make you the trainer of your own body so that you can go or now stay anywhere and feel confident &nbsp;to exercise correctly. My goal is to help my client find their true why (why do they want to be fit, why do they want to eat better) and then cater a plan to help them reach that why. I am extremely client focused and work to the best of my ability to make sure the client needs always come first. We have one body in this lifetime, why not make it the best body it can be!</p>\n<p><br />My training philosophy is to empower those who are ready in their journey to self improvement by prioritizing physical fitness and health nutrition.<br /><br />In a typical training session, I will use the \"tell, show, do\" model. I will tell the client what exercise to perform and why, show the client how to do the exercise, and then have the client perform the exercise while making the necessary corrections in form or posture. Jonathan became passionate about movement from a young age. As a child, he danced for the Miami City Ballet as well as participated in competitive figure skating and swimming. In college, he swam on the college team, he was a member of the ballroom dance team at Florida Atlantic University, and coached the master&rsquo;s swimming program. These experiences with movement ignited a curiosity within Jonathan to learn the biomechanics and physiology of movement. Jonathan has earned certifications from ACSM (American College of Sports Medicine) and is certified in: Advanced Healthcare and Fitness Specialist Weight Management Specialist through ACE (American Council on Exercise) Functional Aging Specialist Certificate from the Functional Aging Institute (FAI) TRX Sports Medicine and Functional Training, ViPR, Kettlebell, and Sandbell certified trainer. Jonathan has been attending the IDEA Health and Fitness Association conferences annually since 2009. At these conferences, he looks forward to learning new ways to train clients to prevent and correct injury as well as develop a healthier lifestyle. In 2012, he attended the inaugural mentorship of the Institute of Motion (IOM), where he studied the science behind movement including sleep, cell health, longevity, and fascia. Jonathan is constantly reading books and scholarly journals within the fields of exercise and nutrition. He loves to learn about these subjects because he believes that proper exercise and nutrition are preventative medicine.</p>\n\nCore and Balance\n\nNutrition\n\nStrength Training\n\nPosture\n\nTRX\n\nFunctional Training\n\nSports Performance Training\n\nCross Training\n\nInjury Prevention\n\nWellness\n\nCorrective Exercise\n\nBeginners\n\nMiddle-aged\n\nPost-rehab\n\nRehab\n\nSeniors\n\nStudent-athletes\n\nAddiction\n\nADHD\n\nAnkle Injury\n\nAnxiety\n\nArthritis\n\nAsperger's Syndrome\n\nAsthma\n\nAutism\n\nBack Pain/Issues\n\nBad Posture\n\nBone/Joint Injury\n\nCancer\n\nCardiac Rehab\n\nCeliac Disease\n\nCerebral Palsy\n\nChronic Pain\n\nDementia\n\nDepression\n\nDiabetes\n\nDialysis\n\nDowns Syndrome\n\nEpilepsy\n\nGolfers Elbow\n\nHeadaches/Migraines\n\nHeart Attack\n\nHernia\n\nHigh Blood Pressure\n\nHigh Cholesterol\n\nHypertension\n\nHypothyroidism\n\nKidney Disease\n\nLimited Mobility\n\nLower Body Pain/Issues\n\nMultiple Sclerosis\n\nMuscular Dystrophy\n\nNeuropathy\n\nObesity\n\nPiriformis Syndrome\n\nPostural Issues\n\nPrediabetes\n\nRotator Cuff Issues\n\nSciatica\n\nScoliosis\n\nShoulder Issues\n\nSleep Apnea\n\nStress\n\nStroke recovery\n\nTennis Elbow\n\nSwimming Races\n\nWeddings\n\nTRX Sports Medicine\n\nTRX Certified Functional Trainer\n\nViPR Certified Personal Trainer\n\nACE Certified Personal Trainer\n\nACE Certified Medical Exercise Specialist\n\nACSM Certified Personal Trainer\n\nACE Certified Health Coach\n\nTRX-Rip Trainer\n\nNASM Certified Personal Trainer\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$134\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nJonathan Mastin has been a Health and Wellness Coach serving clients in the Miami, FL area since 2008. I knew from family history that I was an ideal candidate for Type II diabetes if I didn't do anything. I weighed 215 pounds and was carrying around 33% body fat. It was also around this time that I was treated for prostate cancer. Cancer and heart disease also run in my family. So I knew I had to do something!<br><br>I started a regular workout routine, which helped with the blood pressure, but not my weight. Once I started tracking all of my food and drinks, I started to see how bad my diet was. I decided to get that under control and I started losing the weight. After 10 months of restricting my calorie intake, I got down to 155 pounds but I was still too \"fat.\" I then spent the next year focusing on weight training while continuing to monitor and track my calories, focusing on protein. I am finally where I want to be at 160 pounds and 11% body fat.<br><br>I want to help you become the best that you can be. No matter where you are starting, we will set attainable goals to get you to where you want to be. If you do the work, you will get the reward. I’m going to tell you about one of the hardest experie...\n+Read More\n\nAs a coach for others, some people like to think that I have it all together. I had to go through some<br>extreme adversity in order to become the person that I am today. <br><br><br>I’m going to tell you about one of the hardest experiences that I have ever had to go through.<br>When I was 21 years old, I ruptured 2 discs in my vertebrae ending my athletic career, forever<br>changing the rest of my life. Being an athlete, and playing football was all I knew. It was my identity.<br>It was everything to me, and just like that it was gone. I remember lying in bed weeks after surgery,<br>still hardly being able to move, wanting nothing more than to just be normal again. <br>I was depressed from losing my identity and had no motivation. It was honestly one of the hardest<br>things I have ever gone through. After months of bed rest, I was finally released to work out. Having<br>nothing to train towards, I just thought to myself “what’s the point?” I wasn’t eating, I wasn’t training, I<br>felt awful. I needed to make a change. I decided to start eating right, fueling my body the way it<br>needed to be fed. I started training within my limitations, doing what I could, accepting my injury,<br>seeing it as a challenge not a limitation. I became more mindful of who I was, what I needed to do<br>to be happy; that was treating my body like a temple.<br><br>When I decided to make these changes, I started eating right and providing my body with the nutrients it<br>needed to heal and recover. I started meditating everyday, practicing mindfulness to help overcome<br>negative emotions. Most important of all, I started to focus on helping others in their fitness journeys,<br>using my knowledge to get them anywhere they wanted to go! Even though I had barriers in my<br>fitness goals, there was nothing keeping me from helping others to reach theirs!<br>As a result of making these changes, I started to feel empowered. I started to be happy again. I<br>started to have energy and motivation. I was finally feeling like myself again, excited to find my new<br>Identity. <br><br>Here I am years later, and that’s why I decided to become a coach for others. I want you to know<br>that if you’re struggling, going through a hard time, going through adversity, that you’re not<br>alone. I’ve been there, and we have your back here at Altered Fitness. <br><br> After finishing my college football career, I decided to pursue the sport of bodybuilding to truly<br>understand every aspect of the weight loss journey from a physical and mental standpoint. This was<br>one of the most eye-opening experiences I have had the opportunity to do, and learned<br>more about myself than I ever thought I could. I am devoted to educating people on how to exercise safely to meet their goals while minimizing the r...\n+Read More\n\n<p>My name is Amanda Jessop, and Channel Your Inner Athlete is a mobile personal training company located in Amarillo, TX. I am devoted to educating people on how to exercise safely to meet their goals while minimizing the risk of injury through personalized &amp; balanced private, semi-private, and group training sessions in the comfort of their own home or local parks.<br /><br />My goal is to change the way you look at personal training, in that personal training is not just getting a great workout, but to educate and equip you with the knowledge you need to take action to exercise safely, develop body awareness, and prevent exercise related injuries long term.<br /><br />I believe everyone should be able to live an active, balanced, pain free life. I view myself as a partner with my customers and my community to help spread the &lsquo;gospel of injury preventive and corrective fitness&rsquo;.<br /><br />Growing up, I was very self-conscious of my looks. I believed that the more attractive you were, the more people liked you and the more successful you were. I had zero self-confidence and battled with depression and anxiety. Exercise became my release and daily practice to de-stress and forget about everything in my life.<br /><br />So I started lifting weights and running A LOT. Everything exercise related I did to the extreme. Run 10 miles everyday, lift for 2 hours, etc. I focused more and more on improving my strengths, and as I did this my weaknesses got weaker. I started developing many overuse injuries: multiple leg stress fractures, patella femoral and mal-tracking problems in my knee, meniscus tear, etc.<br /><br />Because of this, my depression got worse. When I was injured I couldn&rsquo;t exercise, and that was my outlet. Not only that, but my negative mindset started effecting other areas of my life including: career, family, relationships, etc. I ended up having surgery on both knees, because every time I went into my doctor, he could never tell me HOW to prevent these injuries from happening. He was just there for the quick fix. And I FINALLY didn&rsquo;t take that as an answer. I very well knew other people had flat feet including athletes, that overcame and prevented their injuries and didn&rsquo;t resort to surgery.<br /><br />So I started studying how athletes train and followed all the big functional athletic coaches, including Todd Durkin, Mike Boyle, and Eric Cressey, Gray Cook, etc.<br /><br />I then applied these injury preventative principles to my own fitness and developed the hands down most IMPORTANT thing anyone can learn: Body Awareness. What I also learned was that PAIN is our body telling us we aren&rsquo;t moving properly. With this knowledge I attained, I have been injury and pain free ever since. My journey consisting of enduring injury setbacks, overcoming them, and then learning how to prevent them is what I term CHANNELING MY INNER ATHLETE. Now it&rsquo;s your turn!</p>\n\nCore and Balance\n\nStrength Training\n\nFlexibility and Mobility\n\nPosture\n\nFunctional Training\n\nSports Performance Training\n\nInjury Prevention\n\nCorrective Exercise\n\nMiddle-aged\n\nSeniors\n\nWomen\n\nYouth\n\nAnkle Injury\n\nArtificial Hips\n\nAsthma\n\nBack Pain/Issues\n\nHigh Blood Pressure\n\nNeck Pain\n\nObesity\n\nParkinson's Disease\n\nPiriformis Syndrome\n\nPlantar fasciitis\n\nRotator Cuff Issues\n\nSciatica\n\nBaseball\n\nBasketball\n\nSoccer\n\nTennis\n\nFunctional Movement Systems- FMS L1 & L2\n\nCPR/First Aid\n\nNSCA Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist\n\nB.S. Starting out in the health industry as a medical assistant years back, I pivoted my focus to the natural/holist...\n+Read More\n\n<p>Aho, a warm welcome to you! Originally from Palm Springs, California, I now reside nestled in Eugene, Oregon. Starting out in the health industry as a medical assistant years back, I pivoted my focus to the natural/holistic side of health a few years ago and decided to study exercise and nutrition since they have both had such a direct impact on my life. I am truly passionate about helping anyone who feels the inevitable pull to improve upon their body and/or mind. I believe it is easier than most people think not only to alter your body composition, lose fat and gain muscle, but to create lasting change and reformation across any and every outlet imaginable. She’s been working with clients for more than 15 years. She began training clients while earning her degree in Exercise Science. Early in her career, she found herself drawn to working with older adults. For her, the most rewarding part of training is seeing someone be able to do what they love.<br>Over the years, Autumn has helped clients overcome the fear of falling and given them ability to get up when they fall. She’s assisted clients in losing weight, which ultimately reduced their medications. Autumn is also an expert at helping clients increase strength and mobility so they can do things they haven’t been able to do for years. <br>Creating a sense of community<br>Autumn’s small group classes bring people together socially and help them build new connections. I host a safe, body-positive space - especially for women, the queer community, and those underrepresented by the \"mainstream\" - where you may challenge yourself to meet athletic goals. You can expect calm, attentive communication from me, with strong encouragement to help you maintain intensity and focus. The primary goals of our training are your safety and a pain-free experience. My duties as a Corpsman required me to have an in depth comprehension of the human body and to treat all types of different injuries, on and off the field. During my time in the service, I worked in a physical therapy clinic in Camp Pendelton, CA. Watching as these Physical Therapist worked with the patients brought me such a sense of fulfilment. I knew that this is what I wanted to do. Health and fitness is not about a number on a scale or a picture in a magazine. Fitness is about feeling good and being the best version of YOU. I create individual programs designed to meet the unique needs of my clients. My clients are BUSY and looking to make changes that make sense for their lifestyle. They LOVE the ease of virtual training. They can click on and off at work, home or vacation. They love learning health and wellness strategies that they can take with them for a lifetime. Growing up, I was never thin. Saddled with adjectives like thick, chunky and&hellip;fat, I learned to loathe my body from an early age. After years of over exercising and dieting, I was NOT healthy and certainly not FIT. Now, as a mother of 3, it is more important than ever to share what I have learned about body acceptance and self-esteem. I teach health and fitness strategies that people can practice for life. I want my children and my clients to love themselves and treat their bodies as the greatest gift ever given, because at the end of the day, your greatest asset is your health.</p>\n\nCore and Balance\n\nNutrition\n\nStrength Training\n\nWeight Loss\n\nFlexibility and Mobility\n\nMuscular Definition\n\nPosture\n\nFunctional Training\n\nKickboxing\n\nCardiovascular Conditioning\n\nCross Training\n\nStress Reduction\n\nWellness\n\nActors\n\nBariatric\n\nBeginners\n\nBrides-to-be\n\nCancer Survivors\n\nDancers\n\nDisabled\n\nLGBTQIA\n\nMen\n\nMiddle-aged\n\nModels\n\nOverweight/ Obese\n\nPeri/Post-menopausal\n\nPre/post-natal\n\nRunners\n\nSeniors\n\nWomen\n\nYouth\n\nADHD\n\nAnkle Injury\n\nAnxiety\n\nArthritis\n\nAsthma\n\nBack Pain/Issues\n\nChronic Pain\n\nStress\n\nBoot Camp\n\nRunning Races (Marathons)\n\nWeddings\n\nCPR/AED/First Aid\n\nISSA Certified Fitness Trainer\n\nISSA Certified Online Coach I\n\nISSA Certified Fitness Coach\n\nNAFC Certified Group X\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$29\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nA fitness coach, personal trainer and Group X instructor with over 30 years of experience in fitness, I have learned there is no \"One Size Fits All\" fitness program because no t...\n+Read More\n\n$59\n\nPer Session\n\nCarlos Matias\n\nVirtual Training Only\n\nEST\n\nI designed a Kickboxing workout to help manage your weight and improve your cardio with an easy-to-follow fun program. I offer the tools and instruction, and with them you build your healthiest self. <br><br>I'm a certified personal trainer and mobility specialist with a background in nursing, and a firm believer that mindset, movement, and nutrition are the pillars for healthy and happy living. While working with me, you can expect guidance in improving all 3 of these pillars. <br><br>I have been working in the fitness industry for 8 years. My career started while I was a fitness instructor in Boston throughout nursing school, and it was here that I learned proactive and preventive healthcare/treatment was my passion rather than reactive management. Upon graduation in 2018, I left conventional medicine and became a personal trainer to pursue my dream of empowering people to help themselves become stronger in every sense, and stay out of the hospital. I worked as a Boston Equinox personal trainer for nearly 2 years, and started my own business Life Specific Fitness in late 2019. <br><br>I'm probably one of the nerdiest personal trainers out there. I value education and emphasize that I have spent years diving into certifications and graduate courses on health, wellness, and fitness. If you are looking for a simple push/pull program or 5x5, I am not your woman. If you are looking for impacting your health, making lifestyle changes in areas such as stress management, sleep, hydration, and mobility, then you are on the right profile. If you are rehabbing an acute, chronic, or recurrent injury, or if you are looking to prevent injury, then you are also on the right profile. <br><br>With my knowledge from nursing, group fitness, advanced certifications, and neuromusculoskeletal health from my current chiropractic program, I am confident in helping clients with a range of complications, comorbidities, and fitness levels. My training style includes mobility and stability work, proprioception and mind-body connection, fascial sling training, mental and physical strength, and, most importantly, how to become a healthier human. <br><br>I typically work with clients in their homes, virtually, or outdoors, though I do have gym space available. <br><br>I stress to all of my clients that I am their guide on their health and wellness journey, and they are on the front lines. Invest in your health, because investing in your illness or injury is never anyone's goal. Whether you are interested in weight loss or stress reduction, obstacle course racing or achieving your first pull-up, and so on, I look forward to helping you achieve your own life specific fitness.\n\nCore and Balance\n\nFlexibility and Mobility\n\nPosture\n\nFunctional Training\n\nCardiovascular Conditioning\n\nInjury Prevention\n\nStress Reduction\n\nWellness\n\nBeginners\n\nBrides-to-be\n\nDancers\n\nLGBTQIA\n\nMiddle-aged\n\nOverweight/ Obese\n\nPeri/Post-menopausal\n\nPost-rehab\n\nPre/post-natal\n\nRehab\n\nRunners\n\nSeniors\n\nStudent-athletes\n\nWomen\n\nACL tear\n\nADHD\n\nAmputation\n\nAnkle Injury\n\nAnxiety\n\nArthritis\n\nArtificial Hips\n\nArtificial Knees\n\nAutism\n\nAutoimmune Diseases\n\nBack Pain/Issues\n\nBad Posture\n\nBone/Joint Injury\n\nBrain Trauma\n\nBreast Cancer\n\nCancer\n\nCardiac Rehab\n\nCeliac Disease\n\nChronic Pain\n\nCongestive Heart Failure\n\nCrohn's Disease\n\nDepression\n\nDiabetes\n\nDiastasis Recti\n\nEhlers Danlos Syndrome (EDS)\n\nFibromyalgia\n\nGolfers Elbow\n\nHamstring Issues\n\nHeadaches/Migraines\n\nHernia\n\nHip Pain/Issues\n\nHypertension\n\nHyperthyroidism\n\nHypothyroidism\n\nJoint Pain\n\nJoint Replacement\n\nKnee Pain/Issues\n\nLimited Mobility\n\nLower Body Pain/Issues\n\nNeck Pain\n\nObesity\n\nOsteoporosis\n\nPiriformis Syndrome\n\nPlantar fasciitis\n\nPostural Issues\n\nPregnancy\n\nRheumatoid Arthritis\n\nRotator Cuff Issues\n\nSciatica\n\nScoliosis\n\nShoulder Issues\n\nSleep Apnea\n\nStress\n\nStroke recovery\n\nTennis Elbow\n\nTMJ\n\nNASM CES\n\nTRX Level 1\n\nFRC Mobility Specialist\n\nFMS Level 1\n\nProNatal Fitness Pre/Postnatal Certified\n\nCPR/AED/First Aid\n\nMyofascial Compression Techniques\n\nTrigger Point\n\nNASM Certified Personal Trainer\n\nBachelor of Science in Nursing from Northeastern University\n\nCandidate for Doctor of Chiropractic from University of Bridgeport\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$29\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nHi, I'm Rebekah! Fitness is my therapy, in the purest sense. For me, fitness has become a source of mental release, physical perseverance, and literally a source release of positive energy. I hope to share that positive energy with you through physical fitness and wellness.<br><br>What I mean by “wellness,” is that I have come to realize that health goes beyond the work I do in the gym and the physical results I get from it. I have learned, over time, that “being healthy” means I have to be mentally, emotionally, as well as physically well. For me, fitness helps me meet all of those needs. I FEEL better when I work out. It is my safe space, and because of that, I get so much more out of it all than abs… although those are nice too.<br><br> My day begins at four A.M. Each morning…I believe in giving the first part of the day to myself. I own it and it begins with physical fitness, I leave it ALL there, no excuses. I felt that I was pushed towards a body standard that I would not be able to compete with. I eventually found an activity that I actually felt confident in, lifting weights. As I started focusing on PERFORMANCE-based goals instead of SCALE-based goals, I found value in myself and my ability to be \"fit\". This ideology has stuck with me throughout my career, and I wish for all of my clients to find confidence in their bodies through learning proper movement mechanics and different forms of exercise. Together we will learn habits of consistency and MAKE IT HAPPEN!! -BS Kinesiology, 2021 -ACE Certified Personal Trainer 2020</p>\n\nCore and Balance\n\nStrength Training\n\nWeight Loss\n\nTRX\n\nFunctional Training\n\nSports Performance Training\n\nBeginners\n\nBrides-to-be\n\nMen\n\nPost-rehab\n\nWomen\n\nArtificial Knees\n\nDiabetes\n\nBoot Camp\n\nOlympic Weightlifting\n\nPowerlifting Competitions\n\nSwimming Races\n\nWeddings\n\nACE Certified Personal Trainer\n\nKinesiology and Health Promotion\n\n1-on-1 Training from\n\n$29\n\nPer Session\n\nView Profile\n\nThroughout my childhood, I hated the idea of exercise and always felt like I had a bad relationship with my body.
